review.summary: The series appears correct. Patch 1 fixes a real stale-state bug in HPM timer reprogramming by resetting irq_overflow_left before recomputing the next expiry, and patch 2 brings IPSR.PMIP handling in line with the existing IPSR recomputation path so software can clear the PM interrupt-pending bit via the normal RW1C write flow. I did not find functional regressions in the touched paths from the provided diffs and surrounding upstream logic.

stages.A: Cover letter and per-patch intent are consistent with the code changes. The series is narrowly scoped and each patch addresses one concrete issue.
stages.B: Patch 1 matches the timer callback/setup design: irq_overflow_left is consumed asynchronously and must be cleared before scheduling a new overflow window, otherwise a prior large-overflow residue can incorrectly reschedule the next callback.
stages.C: Patch 2 is consistent with the IPSR update model used for CIP/FIP/PIP. MMIO writes first apply WC semantics, then riscv_iommu_update_ipsr recomputes pending bits from the resulting register value and source state; adding PMIP to that recomputation path is the missing piece for RW1C behavior.
stages.D: No new API or ABI surface is introduced. The main residual risk is lack of explicit test coverage in the submission for PMIP clear behavior and for reprogramming the HPM timer after a previously split overflow, but I did not identify a code defect in the implementation itself.
stages.E: Recommendation: accept as-is. Optional follow-up would be targeted qtests for IPSR.PMIP write-clear behavior and HPM timer reprogramming across INT64_MAX split scheduling.

cover_letter:
Subject: [PATCH v2 0/2] Bug fixes and IPSR.PMIP support
From: Jay Chang <jay.chang@sifive.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Mar 2026 13:00:09 +0800
Message-Id: <20260325050011.66722-1-jay.chang@sifive.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

This series contains two fixes for the RISC-V IOMMU implementation:

1. Fix a bug in the HPM (Hardware Performance Monitor) timer setup where
   irq_overflow_left was not properly reset, causing stale values from
   previous timer setups to affect new timer behavior.

2. Add proper RW1C (Read/Write 1 to Clear) support for the IPSR.PMIP
   (Performance Monitor Interrupt Pending) bit, which was missing from
   the IPSR register implementation.

---
Change in V2
  Add commit message

Jay Chang (2):
  hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm: Fix irq_overflow_left residual value bug
  hw/riscv/riscv-iommu: Add IPSR.PMIP RW1C support

 hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-bits.h | 1 +
 hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm.c  | 1 +
 hw/riscv/riscv-iommu.c      | 4 ++++
 3 files changed, 6 insertions(+)

-- 
2.48.1
diff:
From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH v2 1/2] hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm: Fix irq_overflow_left
 residual value bug
From: Jay Chang <jay.chang@sifive.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Mar 2026 13:00:10 +0800
Message-Id: <20260325050011.66722-2-jay.chang@sifive.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Reset irq_overflow_left to 0 before setting up a new timer. Without
this fix, a stale irq_overflow_left value from a previous timer setup
could cause incorrect timer behavior.

Signed-off-by: Jay Chang <jay.chang@sifive.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Chang <frank.chang@sifive.com>
Reviewed-by: Chao Liu <chao.liu.zevorn@gmail.com>
---
 hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm.c | 1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

diff --git a/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm.c b/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm.c
index c5034bff79..e8d284ac8b 100644
--- a/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm.c
+++ b/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-hpm.c
@@ -228,6 +228,7 @@ static void hpm_setup_timer(RISCVIOMMUState *s, uint64_t value)
     }
 
     overflow_at = (uint64_t)qemu_clock_get_ns(QEMU_CLOCK_VIRTUAL) + overflow_ns;
+    s->irq_overflow_left = 0;
 
     if (overflow_at > INT64_MAX) {
         s->irq_overflow_left = overflow_at - INT64_MAX;
-- 
2.48.1

From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH v2 2/2] hw/riscv/riscv-iommu: Add IPSR.PMIP RW1C support
From: Jay Chang <jay.chang@sifive.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Mar 2026 13:00:11 +0800
Message-Id: <20260325050011.66722-3-jay.chang@sifive.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Add proper RW1C (Read/Write 1 to Clear) support for the IPSR.PMIP
(Performance Monitor Interrupt Pending) bit, which was missing from
the IPSR register implementation.

Signed-off-by: Jay Chang <jay.chang@sifive.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Chang <frank.chang@sifive.com>
Reviewed-by: Nutty Liu <nutty.liu@hotmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Chao Liu <chao.liu.zevorn@gmail.com>
---
 hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-bits.h | 1 +
 hw/riscv/riscv-iommu.c      | 4 ++++
 2 files changed, 5 insertions(+)

diff --git a/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-bits.h b/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-bits.h
index 47fe01bee5..a938fd3eb4 100644
--- a/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-bits.h
+++ b/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu-bits.h
@@ -189,6 +189,7 @@ enum riscv_iommu_ddtp_modes {
 #define RISCV_IOMMU_REG_IPSR            0x0054
 #define RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_CIP            BIT(0)
 #define RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_FIP            BIT(1)
+#define RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PMIP           BIT(2)
 #define RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PIP            BIT(3)
 
 enum {
diff --git a/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu.c b/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu.c
index 225394ea83..f8b5187328 100644
--- a/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu.c
+++ b/hw/riscv/riscv-iommu.c
@@ -2153,6 +2153,10 @@ static void riscv_iommu_update_ipsr(RISCVIOMMUState *s, uint64_t data)
         ipsr_clr |= RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_FIP;
     }
 
+    if (!(data & RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PMIP)) {
+        ipsr_clr |= RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PMIP;
+    }
+
     if (data & RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PIP) {
         pqcsr = riscv_iommu_reg_get32(s, RISCV_IOMMU_REG_PQCSR);
 
-- 
2.48.1
